explicit_text: I was born in Meiji 23, which is 1890, during the Meiji period in Japan. I came to America in Meiji 32, when I was 19 years old. This period was a time of great change in Japan, and it influenced my decision to seek a future in America. My family were farmers back in Japan, and I only went to junior high school before coming here.
implicit_text: I was born in the 23rd year of the era that started in 1868, which is 1890, during a time of great modernization in Japan. I came to America in the 32nd year of that era, when I was 19 years old. This era was a time of great change in Japan, and it influenced my decision to seek a future in America. My family were farmers back in Japan, and I only went to junior high school before coming here.

explicit_text: I arrived in San Francisco when I first came to America in 1899. I stayed at a Japanese boarding house there and had a friend who looked after me. I also went through Angel Island for two days before settling. San Francisco was home to about 200 Japanese at that time, and many Japanese laundries and dry cleaning businesses were established there.
implicit_text: I arrived in the major West Coast city known for its bay when I first came to America in 1899. I stayed at a Japanese boarding house there and had a friend who looked after me. I also went through the immigration station on an island in the bay for two days before settling. That city was home to about 200 Japanese at that time, and many Japanese laundries and dry cleaning businesses were established there.

explicit_text: A man named Mr. Domoto from my hometown operated North American, and he was the one who encouraged me to come to America. I came on the same ship with him and his bride. He told me there was a great future here, which motivated my journey.
implicit_text: A man named Mr. Domoto from my hometown operated a newspaper serving Japanese immigrants in the United States, and he was the one who encouraged me to come to America. I came on the same ship with him and his bride. He told me there was a great future here, which motivated my journey.

explicit_text: In 1907, after the 1906 San Francisco earthquake, I bought 5 acres of land on contract in Redwood City to grow flowers. I faced challenges due to the Alien Land Law later, but I managed to keep the land by forming the Enomoto Company with my family. Redwood City became the base of my flower business, which my children and grandchildren continue to operate today.
implicit_text: In 1907, after the 1906 earthquake in the nearby major city, I bought 5 acres of land on contract in a city south of San Francisco to grow flowers. I faced challenges due to a restrictive land ownership law later, but I managed to keep the land by forming a family corporation. This city became the base of my flower business, which my children and grandchildren continue to operate today.

explicit_text: The 1906 San Francisco earthquake had a big impact on me because I had to leave the area due to the fire after the quake. It was a difficult time, but soon after, I was able to buy land in Redwood City and start my flower growing business. The earthquake indirectly influenced my move and business decisions.
implicit_text: The major earthquake and subsequent fire in the San Francisco area in 1906 had a big impact on me because I had to leave the area due to the fire after the quake. It was a difficult time, but soon after, I was able to buy land in a neighboring city and start my flower growing business. The disaster indirectly influenced my move and business decisions.

explicit_text: Around 1910, the Alien Land Law was enacted, preventing noncitizens like me from buying land. Since I already owned land, I was allowed to keep it, but I couldn’t transfer it to my wife if I died. To get around this, we formed a corporation called the Enomoto Company with my wife and child, which allowed us to hold and expand the land legally.
implicit_text: Around 1910, a law was enacted in California preventing noncitizens like me from buying land. Since I already owned land, I was allowed to keep it, but I couldn’t transfer it to my wife if I died. To get around this, we formed a corporation with my wife and child, which allowed us to hold and expand the land legally.

explicit_text: The Enomoto Company was formed by myself, my wife, and my child to hold our land jointly and avoid issues with the Alien Land Law. This joint stock company allowed our family to maintain and expand our flower growing business. The company still operates today, managed by my children and grandchildren.
implicit_text: The family corporation was formed by myself, my wife, and my child to hold our land jointly and avoid issues with the restrictive land ownership law. This joint stock company allowed our family to maintain and expand our flower growing business. The company still operates today, managed by my children and grandchildren.

explicit_text: We expanded our flower growing operations to Half Moon Bay, where my daughter’s family now runs a large nursery of about 30 acres. This area became an important part of our family’s horticultural business alongside Redwood City. Half Moon Bay represents the growth and continuation of our nursery legacy.
implicit_text: We expanded our flower growing operations to a coastal city south of San Francisco, where my daughter’s family now runs a large nursery of about 30 acres. This area became an important part of our family’s horticultural business alongside our original city. This coastal location represents the growth and continuation of our nursery legacy.

explicit_text: I knew Dr. Sturge, who was the superintendent of the Japanese Presbyterian Churches before he died around 1917. He was friendly to Japanese people and helped me learn English, which was very important for my business and life here. His support made a big difference in my early years in America.
implicit_text: I knew Dr. Sturge, who was the superintendent of the Presbyterian churches serving Japanese immigrants before he died around 1917. He was friendly to Japanese people and helped me learn English, which was very important for my business and life here. His support made a big difference in my early years in America.

explicit_text: During World War II, when I was interned in North Dakota, my wife and five children moved to Denver, Colorado. They rented a house and stayed there until the war ended. After my hearing, I joined them in Denver, but I didn’t do much there except receive Social Security and watch my daughter work as a stenographer.
implicit_text: During World War II, when I was interned in North Dakota, my wife and five children moved to a city in Colorado. They rented a house and stayed there until the war ended. After my hearing, I joined them there, but I didn’t do much except receive Social Security and watch my daughter work as a stenographer.

explicit_text: World War II was a difficult time for me and my family. After Pearl Harbor, the FBI investigated my import business and I was interned in North Dakota while my family moved to Denver. We closed the flower business during the war, and I retired near age 60. Despite hardships, I felt the government took good care of us in the camps and understood it was wartime.
implicit_text: The global conflict from 1939 to 1945 was a difficult time for me and my family. After the attack on the U.S. naval base in Hawaii, the FBI investigated my import business and I was interned in North Dakota while my family moved to Colorado. We closed the flower business during the war, and I retired near age 60. Despite hardships, I felt the government took good care of us in the camps and understood it was wartime.

explicit_text: My family chose not to enter the War Relocation Authority centers during the evacuation order in World War II. Instead, they moved to Denver on their own. I was interned in a government-controlled camp in North Dakota, which was orderly and well-managed. The WRA centers were part of the broader internment system, but my family avoided them by relocating independently.
implicit_text: My family chose not to enter the government-run internment centers during the evacuation order in World War II. Instead, they moved to Colorado on their own. I was interned in a government-controlled camp in North Dakota, which was orderly and well-managed. The official relocation centers were part of the broader internment system, but my family avoided them by relocating independently.

explicit_text: During the Great Depression, I was able to maintain my flower business despite many accounts that I couldn’t collect. Some businesses went bankrupt, but I managed to survive with the help of the Anglo California Bank. It was a tough time, but I kept selling about the same amount of flowers and persevered through the economic hardship.
implicit_text: During the severe economic downturn of the 1930s, I was able to maintain my flower business despite many accounts that I couldn’t collect. Some businesses went bankrupt, but I managed to survive with the help of a local American bank. It was a tough time, but I kept selling about the same amount of flowers and persevered through the economic hardship.

explicit_text: The Anglo California Bank was very helpful to me during the Great Depression when I needed financial support. Unlike the Japanese banks, which I did not use, this American bank provided assistance that helped me keep my flower business afloat. I appreciated their support in those difficult times.
implicit_text: A local American bank was very helpful to me during the Great Depression when I needed financial support. Unlike the Japanese banks, which I did not use, this bank provided assistance that helped me keep my flower business afloat. I appreciated their support in those difficult times.

explicit_text: Sumitomo Bank was one of the Japanese banks operating in California when I was doing business. However, I did not use Sumitomo Bank for my flower business because I thought they didn’t loan money for that type of business. Most Japanese firms used the Yokohama Specie Bank instead, but I relied on American banks like Anglo California Bank.
implicit_text: One of the Japanese banks operating in California when I was doing business did not provide loans for my flower business, so I did not use their services. Most Japanese firms used another Japanese bank instead, but I relied on American banks like the one that helped me during the Depression.

explicit_text: The Yokohama Specie Bank was already established in San Francisco when I arrived. Many Japanese firms did business with them, especially in import-export. However, I did not use their services for my flower business because I believed they didn’t provide loans for that type of enterprise. I preferred to work with American banks.
implicit_text: This Japanese bank was already established in San Francisco when I arrived. Many Japanese firms did business with them, especially in import-export. However, I did not use their services for my flower business because I believed they didn’t provide loans for that type of enterprise. I preferred to work with American banks.

explicit_text: I was surprised when the Pearl Harbor attack happened on December 7, 1941, but I knew there was nothing I could do about it. Rumors spread that Japanese Americans would be put in camps, but I didn’t think we would be killed. About a week after the attack, the FBI came to investigate my business, and about a month later, I was taken to an internment camp.
implicit_text: I was surprised when the surprise military strike happened on December 7, 1941, but I knew there was nothing I could do about it. Rumors spread that Japanese Americans would be put in camps, but I didn’t think we would be killed. About a week after the attack, the FBI came to investigate my business, and about a month later, I was taken to an internment camp.

explicit_text: The FBI came to my place about one week after the Pearl Harbor attack to investigate my import business. They checked all my books thoroughly but did not close my business immediately. I wasn’t afraid because I hadn’t done anything illegal. However, about a month later, I was taken to an internment camp.
implicit_text: The federal investigative agency came to my place about one week after the surprise attack on the naval base to investigate my import business. They checked all my books thoroughly but did not close my business immediately. I wasn’t afraid because I hadn’t done anything illegal. However, about a month later, I was taken to an internment camp.

explicit_text: My eldest son was studying at the University of California when the war broke out. He quit school to help me with the flower business but returned after the war and graduated. The University played an important role in my family’s education and future success.
implicit_text: My eldest son was studying at a major public university in California when the war broke out. He quit school to help me with the flower business but returned after the war and graduated. The university played an important role in my family’s education and future success.

explicit_text: I was born in Kagoshima, Japan, but I was raised in Manchuria when I was young. I have been to Manchuria once as an adult before the war, working for a firm there. My early experiences in Manchuria gave me some perspective before coming to America.
implicit_text: I was born in Kagoshima, Japan, but I was raised in the northeastern region of China when I was young. I have been to that region once as an adult before the war, working for a firm there. My early experiences there gave me some perspective before coming to America.

explicit_text: When I arrived in San Francisco, I went through Angel Island and stayed there for two days. It was the immigration station for many Asian immigrants like myself. After that, I went to a Japanese boarding house in San Francisco to settle in.
implicit_text: When I arrived in the San Francisco Bay Area, I went through the immigration station on an island in the bay and stayed there for two days. It was the immigration station for many Asian immigrants like myself. After that, I went to a Japanese boarding house in the city to settle in.

explicit_text: I attended Heald College in the evenings to improve my English and business skills. This helped me manage my flower business better and communicate effectively. Learning English was essential for my success in America.
implicit_text: I attended a business college in the evenings to improve my English and business skills. This helped me manage my flower business better and communicate effectively. Learning English was essential for my success in America.

explicit_text: I was born in Kagoshima Prefecture, Japan, and grew up there before moving to Manchuria as a child. My family were farmers in Kagoshima, and I left there at age 19 to come to America. My roots in Kagoshima shaped my early life and values.

My family were farmers there, and I left at age 19 to come to America. My roots in that prefecture shaped my early life and values.
